title: Fresh Velvety Indian Lamb Dal
description: A comforting, slow-cooked Indian dish featuring tender lamb and a hint of sweetness from frozen mixed berries.

ingredients.name: 1 pound boneless lamb shoulder or neck, cut into 2-inch pieces
ingredients.notes: Look for lamb with a good balance of fat and lean meat.

ingredients.name: 1 medium onion, diced
ingredients.notes: Use a sweet onion, such as Vidalia or Maui.

ingredients.name: 2 cloves garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount of garlic to your taste.

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon grated fresh ginger
ingredients.notes: Make sure to peel the ginger before grating.

ingredients.name: 1 cup frozen mixed berries
ingredients.notes: Choose a mix of sweet and tart berries for a balanced flavor.

ingredients.name: 2 cups water
ingredients.notes: You can also use lamb or beef broth for added flavor.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Use fresh cumin seeds for a more aromatic flavor.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon ground coriander
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount of coriander to your taste.

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality turmeric powder for maximum flavor.

ingredients.name: 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adjust the amount of cayenne pepper to your desired level of heat.

instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the onion to the pot.
Cook the onion until it is lightly browned and caramelized, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the mixed berries, water, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cayenne pepper (if using) to the pot.
Stir to combine, then add the browned lamb back to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the dal hot, garnished with fresh cilantro or scallions, if desired.
